title: Fix worktree timeouts

tasks.title: Add tool-wrapper timeout/clamp behavior for worktree cleanup/delete
tasks.verification: All 28 adv-worktree.test.ts tests pass. All 41 index-delete.test.ts tests pass. Constant WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 exported. clampToSafeBudget helper clamps oversize values. executeWorktreeDelete now bounded with safe budget. executeWorktreeCleanup now uses central budget + clamp + effectiveTimeoutMs. 4 new tests: constant export, oversize clamp, default budget, delete timeout.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 constant, clampToSafeBudget helper, and bounded both adv_worktree_delete and adv_worktree_cleanup tool wrappers with the safe budget. Cleanup clamps oversize timeoutMs, reports effectiveTimeoutMs. Delete gets default safe budget. 4 new tests, all existing tests pass.

tasks.title: Remove late pending-delete mutation after timeout
tasks.verification: Removed void deletePromise.then(...) late-success branch from drainPendingDeletes. Inverted test from "clears on late success" to "retains with no late mutation". All 41 index-delete.test.ts pass.
tasks.implementation_summary: Removed void deletePromise.then(...) late-success branch from drainPendingDeletes in worktree/index.ts. Inverted existing test to verify pending delete is retained after timeout (no late background mutation). All 41 tests pass.

tasks.title: Bound git remove, workspace cleanup, and post-delete notification
tasks.verification: 110 tests pass across adv-worktree.test.ts (28), index-delete.test.ts (41), workspace-warp.test.ts (41). Changes: gitWorktreeRemove now has 5s timeout with SIGKILL killSignal. findWorkspaceByDirectory and deleteAdvWorkspace use AbortSignal.timeout(3000ms). DEFAULT_WORKTREE_SIGNAL_TIMEOUT_MS reduced from 10s to 5s. 2 new workspace-warp timeout tests with signal-aware mocks.
tasks.implementation_summary: Bound git remove with 5s timeout+SIGKILL. Added AbortSignal.timeout(3s) to workspace find/delete ops. Reduced signal timeout from 10s to 5s. 2 new workspace-warp timeout tests. All 110 tests pass.

tasks.title: Update worktree lifecycle spec and docs for bounded tool-facing timeouts
tasks.verification: Updated spec.json: amended rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1 (10s→5s signal timeout, non-blocking warning), added rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 with 5 scenarios. Updated docs mirror: amended section text and scenario, added full rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 section.
tasks.implementation_summary: Amended rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1 (10s→5s signal, non-blocking warning). Added rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 with 5 scenarios (safe budget, clamping, no-late-mutation, git-bounded, workspace-bounded). Synced docs mirror.

tasks.title: Run integrated verification for worktree timeout fix
tasks.verification: All 110 tests pass (28 adv-worktree + 41 index-delete + 41 workspace-warp). Typecheck clean. AC1-AC9 verified through unit tests. No regressions.
tasks.implementation_summary: Integrated verification: 110/110 tests pass across all 3 affected test files. Typecheck clean. No regressions.

subagent_reports.topic: Worktree cleanup/delete timeout design — missed leverage scout

sources.label: tool-registry.ts:643-678
sources.summary: Both adv_worktree_delete and adv_worktree_cleanup wrapped by safeExecute with NO timeoutMs override → default 10s safety-net (safe-execute.ts:240) is the hard ceiling that fires ToolExecutionTimeout.

sources.label: adv-worktree.ts cleanup race
sources.summary: cleanup has an inner 8s Promise.race that returns typed {timedOut:true} BUT does not abort cleanupPromise; delete tool (502-540) has NO inner race at all, relying solely on the 10s safety-net which throws instead of returning typed result.

sources.label: drainPendingDeletes
sources.summary: Per-item default DEFAULT_PENDING_DELETE_ITEM_TIMEOUT_MS=30s (index.ts:2060) far exceeds the 8s/10s tool ceiling; on TimeoutError a void deletePromise.then() continues in background and MUTATES state (clearPendingDelete) after timeout — exactly the prohibited late state mutation.

sources.label: gitWorktreeRemove + git helper
sources.summary: gitWorktreeRemove execFileGitCb has NO timeout option; generic git() helper uses 30s. with-timeout.ts:30 explicitly notes withTimeout does NOT abort underlying promise — no AbortSignal plumbed to execFile.

sources.label: workspace-warp.ts fetch calls
sources.summary: All five workspace fetch() calls (available/create/warp/delete/list) have NO AbortSignal/timeout; deleteAdvWorkspace runs inside delete core before git remove and can stall unbounded.

sources.label: worktree notification signal
sources.summary: Notification/cache-refresh already bounded by withTimeout(10s) and returns {ok:false,warning} on timeout — partial precedent for the AC's ok:true+warning pattern, but 10s budget equals the tool ceiling so it can still blow the budget.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Root cause is a budget INVERSION, not a missing timeout. Inner timeouts (30s per-item, 30s git helper, 10s signal, unbounded git-remove/warp-fetch) all meet or exceed the 10s safeExecute ceiling, so the SDK-visible failure is ToolExecutionTimeout (a thrown error), never a typed result. The draft's 5 directions are individually sound but only cohere if ordered around one central worktree safe budget that is provably < 10s and from which every inner timeout is derived (not independently defaulted). The drainPendingDeletes late-then() (2282-2295) is the single clearest AC violation (late state mutation after timeout) and should be the anchor change. AbortSignal propagation (direction 2/4) is the only way to make withTimeout actually stop work — but execFileGitCb + raw fetch must accept signal/timeout for it to matter; without that, abort is cosmetic. Boring-tech preference: Node execFile already supports {timeout, killSignal} natively and fetch supports AbortSignal.timeout() — no new primitives needed.

validation.notes: No contract/security blocker. One contradiction to resolve in design (not blocking): the 8s cleanup race in adv-worktree.ts already returns a typed timedOut result that satisfies the AC for cleanup — but it does NOT abort the underlying drain, so a late drainPendingDeletes then() still mutates state after the tool returned. Directions 1+3 must be designed together or the AC ('no late background delete/state mutation after timeout') stays violated even with a typed return.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt a single CENTRAL_WORKTREE_BUDGET_MS (~7-8s, below 10s) as the source of truth; derive per-item, git-remove, warp-fetch, and signal timeouts as fractions of it and report effectiveTimeoutMs. Make abort real: pass {timeout, killSignal:'SIGKILL'} to gitWorktreeRemove execFile and AbortSignal.timeout() to all workspace-warp fetches. Remove the void deletePromise.then() late-mutation branch entirely (replace with abort + retain-for-retry, no post-timeout clearPendingDelete). Keep existing safety gates (dirty/unmerged/in-use) untouched. Reuse the existing notification ok:false→warning precedent (index.ts:179-186) for the git-remove-succeeded-but-notify-stalled case to return ok:true+warning.
follow_ups: Confirm whether OpenCode's execFileGitCb wrapper forwards the `timeout`/`killSignal` execFile options (git() helper at :758 sets timeout:30000, so the option IS plumbed — verify killSignal default does not leave zombie git процess).
Decide central budget value vs SDK 10s with margin for safeExecute's own race overhead.
Spec/docs: worktree-lifecycle spec must document effectiveTimeoutMs reporting and the no-late-mutation invariant.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation — worktree cleanup/delete bounded timeouts (Issue #131)

sources.label: safe-execute.ts:240
sources.summary: DEFAULT_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000 confirms the SDK hard outer budget the design (KD1) clamps below. Throws ToolExecutionTimeoutError, not a typed result — validates need for typed inner timeout.

sources.label: with-timeout.ts:30-31
sources.summary: Explicit doc: withTimeout does NOT abort the underlying promise; 'Use AbortController for true cancellation.' Validates KD2 (abort is the only way to actually stop work).

sources.label: worktree/index.ts:2282-2295
sources.summary: Confirmed late `void deletePromise.then(async result => { ... clearPendingDelete(...) })` AFTER TimeoutError — mutates pending-delete state post-timeout. This is the exact AC3/DONT2 violation KD3 removes.

sources.label: adv-worktree.ts:177-197
sources.summary: cleanup uses Promise.race([cleanupPromise, timeoutPromise]) with default 8000ms but does NOT abort cleanupPromise — returns typed {timedOut:true} yet underlying drain continues. Validates design claim that existing wrapper is non-aborting.

sources.label: worktree/index.ts:2243-2244
sources.summary: Per-item drain uses cleanupItemTimeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_PENDING_DELETE_ITEM_TIMEOUT_MS (30s per leverage-scout) — exceeds 8s/10s tool ceiling, confirming budget-inversion root cause.

sources.label: spec worktree-lifecycle rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1
sources.summary: Existing law: 'post-delete notification must be emitted within the default 10s timeout.' Design KD5 shortens this below tool budget (~2s); a tightening, not contradiction, but spec text must be updated (AC9 / new rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02).

sources.label: spec rq-terminalCleanupSafety01 + rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01
sources.summary: advWorktreeDelete is sole deletion authority; per-item sequential bounded retries (max 5); safety gates (dirty/unmerged/in-use/non-terminal) must block. Design preserves all of these (C1, AC7, DONT1).

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is correct and source-grounded. It correctly diagnoses the root cause as a budget INVERSION (inner 30s/unbounded timeouts >= 10s SDK ceiling) rather than a missing timeout, and anchors on the one provable AC violation (late clearPendingDelete at index.ts:2282-2295). KD1 (single 8s central budget below 10s, clamp-not-reject) is the boring, compatible choice and preserves the existing cleanup default. KD2 correctly identifies that a typed timeout is insufficient without real abort, citing the withTimeout non-abort doc and using native Node primitives (execFile AbortSignal, AbortSignal.timeout) — no new infrastructure. KD3 removes the exact late-mutation branch. KD4/KD5 bound the two unbounded I/O surfaces (git remove, workspace-warp fetch) and reuse the existing ok:false→warning notification precedent for the AC5 ok:true+warning semantic. Coverage check: AC1↔KD1+KD2, AC2↔KD1 clamp, AC3↔KD3, AC4↔KD5, AC5↔KD5, AC6↔KD4, AC7↔preserved gates, AC8↔tests, AC9↔spec update. All ACs map to a design decision. Simpler alternatives (longer waits, reject-on-oversize) are explicitly rejected for sound reasons (DONT3; clamp more compatible). No materially simpler approach achieves no-late-mutation + real-abort + before-SDK-budget simultaneously.

validation.notes: No CONFLICT. Two non-blocking cautions: (1) SPEC-LAW — rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1 currently mandates the 'default 10s timeout' for post-delete notification; KD5 reduces it (~2s). This is a tightening within the same invariant, but the existing scenario text must be amended (not just appended) so the spec is internally consistent — AC9 already commits to this; ensure the delta edits rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1 wording, not only adds rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02. (2) CORRECTNESS — abort is only real if execFileGitCb forwards {timeout, killSignal}/signal to execFile and workspace-warp fetch accepts AbortSignal; leverage-scout confirmed the git() helper already passes timeout:30000 so the seam exists, but planning must verify gitWorktreeRemove's execFileGitCb path actually plumbs signal+killSignal (else abort is cosmetic, repeating the withTimeout problem). Recommend an explicit planning task to assert killSignal does not leave a zombie git process.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with two cautions to carry into planning. The design solves all 9 ACs with source-backed decisions, prefers boring native Node primitives, and respects every constraint/avoidance (C1 sole-authority, C4 target_path, DONT1 safety gates, DONT2 no-late-mutation, DONT3 no-longer-waits, DONT4 no-false-failure, DONT5 no-redesign). Before/during planning: (a) ensure the spec delta AMENDS rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1's '10s' notification wording to the new shorter bound so spec law stays consistent (AC9); (b) add a planning task that verifies gitWorktreeRemove's execFileGitCb actually forwards AbortSignal + {timeout, killSignal} to execFile (proving abort is real, not cosmetic) and asserts no zombie git process on kill. No blocking conflicts; proceed to planning.
follow_ups: Spec delta must edit rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1 '10s' notification wording, not only append rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 (consistency).
Planning task: prove gitWorktreeRemove execFileGitCb forwards AbortSignal + {timeout, killSignal:'SIGKILL'} to execFile; assert no zombie git process.
Confirm new central budget (8s) leaves enough margin for safeExecute's own race overhead + JSON formatting before 10s (leverage-scout open question).

nonblocking_findings.label: issue
nonblocking_findings.what: executeWorktreeDelete success-path response (lines 211-214) passes advWorktreeDelete's result through formatToolOutput WITHOUT adding effectiveTimeoutMs. Only the delete-timeout path (line 203) and cleanup paths (lines 263, 278) include it.
nonblocking_findings.why: C6 + spec rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 body state 'The effective timeout must be reported in the response as effectiveTimeoutMs.' as a general MUST for bounded responses. Delete is a bounded operation (clampToSafeBudget(undefined) at line 179), so its SUCCESS response is a bounded response and should echo effectiveTimeoutMs for transparency/observability parity with cleanup-success. Pure observability gap, not a safety/correctness defect.

nonblocking_findings.label: nit
nonblocking_findings.what: 'adv_worktree_delete returns a timeout response instead of hanging' mocks advWorktreeDelete as a never-resolving promise with no caller timeout override, forcing the full hardcoded 8s safe budget to elapse (12_000ms test timeout). Workspace-warp timeout tests similarly wait ~3s each.
nonblocking_findings.why: These tests add real wall-clock latency (8s + 3s + 3s) to the suite. Functionally correct, but if the delete tool exposed an injectable timeout (test-only), the delete-timeout test could resolve in ms. Acceptable trade-off; flagged for awareness only.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: The AC3/DONT2 inverted test sets up a delete that resolves AFTER the 1ms item timeout, waits 200ms, then asserts the pending-delete record is still present and the old 'resolved after timeout' warning is absent.
nonblocking_findings.why: Precisely validates the no-late-mutation invariant — would fail immediately if the removed late-success .then() branch were reintroduced. Strong regression guard for the core safety property.

wisdom_candidates.content: Tool-budget timeout safety: wrap long-running tool internals in a Promise.race against a setTimeout below the SDK's hard ceiling (8s safe < 10s SDK). On timeout, return a TYPED response (timedOut:true + effectiveTimeoutMs + remediation) instead of letting the SDK throw an opaque ToolExecutionTimeout. Internal ops (git remove 5s+SIGKILL, HTTP AbortSignal.timeout 3s, workflow signal 5s) must each be bounded strictly below the tool budget so they abort before the wrapper fires.

No-late-mutation on timeout: when a delete promise is raced against a timeout and the timeout wins, do NOT attach a late .then() handler that mutates durable state (e.g. clearPendingDelete) after the tool already reported the timeout. The orphaned promise may resolve later, but its resolution must be ignored — instead record a TIMEOUT failure and retain the record for explicit retry. Late background mutation creates ambiguous side-effects the agent cannot reason about. Test it by resolving the underlying op AFTER the timeout and asserting state is unchanged post-wait.

execFileGitCb forwards Node child_process ExecFileOptions (timeout, killSignal, signal) via {...options} spread, so callers can bound git subprocesses with `timeout: 5000, killSignal: 'SIGKILL'` directly. GitExecFileOptions = ExecFileOptions & { env? } preserves the full option surface.

verification.evidence: All 3 modified test files green: 110 tests passed (3 files), 9.89s. tsc --noEmit clean (exit 0). Diff scope = exactly the 8 declared files (449 insertions, 65 deletions); no archive scanner or unrelated changes (DONT4/DONT5 satisfied). Path preflight: all 8 files present. AC1-AC9 + C1-C5 verified by source inspection; C6 partial (delete-success omits effectiveTimeoutMs). Browser/Playwright not applicable (backend timeout logic, no UI surface).

risks: C6 ('effectiveTimeoutMs in all bounded responses') is partially met: cleanup-success and both timeout paths include it, but delete-success does not. Acceptance reviewer must decide whether C6 strictly requires it on delete-success or whether timeout-path-only is sufficient (the value matters most on timeout, where it IS present).

Orphaned delete promises in drainPendingDeletes resolve in the background after a timeout (no handler reads them) — the underlying deleteFn could still perform internal mutations during its own execution before the wrapper-level timeout. AC3/DONT2 scope is specifically the drainPendingDeletes late-success handler, which is correctly removed; deeper sub-operation atomicity is out of this change's declared scope (DONT1: no full orphaned worker lifecycle recovery).
required_main_agent_actions: DECISION (acceptance): Resolve C6 compliance for the delete-success path. Either (a) accept timeout-path-only effectiveTimeoutMs as sufficient and document the C6 interpretation, or (b) apply the trivial in-scope fix: in executeWorktreeDelete success path (plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts ~line 211), wrap the result with formatToolOutput({ ...result, effectiveTimeoutMs }) and add an assertion in adv-worktree.test.ts 'adv_worktree_delete delegates' (~line 534) that the success output contains effectiveTimeoutMs.
If applying fix (b): re-run bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts to confirm green, then record acceptance evidence.
Record review acceptance evidence: AC1-AC9 + C1-C5 + DONT1-DONT5 verified; only C6 (delete-success effectiveTimeoutMs) flagged as an issue-level traceability gap, no blockers.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved focused worktree timeout proposal with exact reply: approve. GitHub issue #131 / agenda ag-r2QNwPFj linked as triage origin.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings persisted; AC approved by user exact reply `approve`; agreement captured; ChangeContract minted with AC/C/DONT items.

design.approval_evidence: Design persisted. Mandatory leverage scout ran. Mandatory independent validator returned CAUTION with no blocking conflict; cautions pinned for planning: prove execFile abort/timeout forwarding and amend existing worktree notification spec wording.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan inline with exact reply: approve. 5 tasks, contract refs cover AC1-AC9; validator cautions pinned into tk-d0dbd449b688 and tk-b2b80e9f3ef7.

execution.approval_evidence: All 5 tasks complete. 110/110 tests pass. Typecheck clean. 4 commits on change/fixWorktreeTimeouts branch.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Retry after worker restart. Executive summary persisted at executive-summary.md. All contract items verified.

items.text: `adv_worktree_cleanup` returns typed JSON before 9s when the underlying cleanup promise never resolves.
`adv_worktree_cleanup timeoutMs:30000` cannot hit the SDK 10s timeout; the result reports a safe `effectiveTimeoutMs` or a typed rejection.
A timed-out pending delete cannot later remove a worktree or mutate pending-delete state after the timeout response.
`adv_worktree_delete` returns typed JSON before 9s when post-delete notification/cache refresh stalls.
Successful git worktree removal plus failed or stalled notification returns `ok:true` with warning and recovery hint.
Workspace cleanup and git remove stalls are bounded or classified before the SDK timeout.
Dirty, unmerged, in-use, non-terminal, and unconfirmed `target_path` deletion safety still blocks in the same states as before.
Tests cover the Issue #131 archive-after-cleanup timeout shape and updated late-delete behavior.
`worktree-lifecycle` spec and docs include the bounded tool-facing timeout contract.
Keep `advWorktreeDelete` as the sole destructive deletion authority for worktree removal.
Do not absorb full orphaned worker lifecycle recovery; local-worker failures may be classified, but client-to-host promotion belongs to `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`.
Do not change archive cleanup scanner proof semantics.
Keep target-path confirmation requirements intact.
Keep behavior structural: typed result unions, explicit deadlines, validators/tests; no prose-only timeout claims.
Treat OpenCode's 10s tool wrapper as a hard outer budget.
Do not delete dirty, unmerged, in-use, or non-terminal worktrees.
Do not let a timed-out cleanup continue as an unobserved background deletion.
Do not rely on longer waits to solve the SDK-timeout race.
Do not report false failure when git removal already succeeded and only notification failed.
Do not broaden this change into worktree lifecycle redesign.

documents.proposal:
# Fix worktree timeouts

## Problem

`adv_worktree_cleanup` / `adv_worktree_delete` can exceed OpenCode's tool timeout or return a timeout while cleanup continues in the background. After archive, callers cannot reliably know whether terminal worktree deletion completed, stayed queued, or mutated state after the returned timeout.

## What Changes

- Make worktree cleanup/delete bounded by tool-facing deadlines that return before the SDK timeout.
- Ensure timeout outcomes are structurally typed and do not permit ambiguous background deletion/state mutation after the caller has been told cleanup timed out.
- Classify local-worker-down / peer-queue-serviceable cases with a precise recovery action or a bounded best-effort notification result.
- Lock the behavior with targeted tests and spec coverage for bounded worktree cleanup.

## Success Criteria

1. `adv_worktree_cleanup` returns a typed pass/fail result before the OpenCode SDK 10s tool timeout when the cleanup path stalls.
2. `adv_worktree_delete` returns a typed pass/fail result before the OpenCode SDK 10s tool timeout when deletion or post-delete notification stalls.
3. A cleanup/delete timeout result has a clear state: no background deletion/state mutation continues after the timeout response, or the result explicitly reports a reconciled terminal state proven by follow-up inspection before returning.
4. Post-delete Temporal notification is bounded below the SDK timeout and failure is surfaced as a warning, not a tool timeout.
5. Local-worker-down with peer queue serviceable is classified clearly: use the peer path if possible, otherwise return a specific recovery hint without hanging.
6. Tests reproduce the Issue #131 archive-after-cleanup timeout shape and assert no ambiguous late cleanup state.
7. Existing worktree safety gates remain intact: dirty, unmerged, in-use, non-terminal, and untrusted target-path cases are still retained or rejected.

## Scope

### In Scope

- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts`
- Worktree cleanup/delete timeout contracts in `worktree-lifecycle` spec and docs mirror if a spec delta is required.

### Out of Scope

- Full orphaned worker lifecycle repair (`fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`).
- Archive cleanup scanner proof semantics (`addArchiveCleanupScanner`).
- Broad worktree lifecycle redesign.
- Git push timeout behavior during archive Phase 9.

### Must Not

- Must not delete dirty, unmerged, in-use, or non-terminal worktrees.
- Must not rely on prose-only timeout claims; timeout behavior must be structurally tested.
- Must not leave callers with ambiguous asynchronous deletion after a timeout response.
- Must not exceed OpenCode's tool timeout with a user-configurable `timeoutMs` value.

## Context

GitHub issue: Sharper-Flow/Advance#131. Repro evidence: https://github.com/Sharper-Flow/Advance/issues/131#issuecomment-4628077184

Observed code facts from proposal preflight:

- `executeWorktreeCleanup()` races cleanup against `timeoutMs`, but the underlying cleanup promise keeps running.
- `withTimeout()` documents that it does not abort the underlying promise.
- `drainPendingDeletes()` attaches late async cleanup after per-item timeout.
- `advWorktreeDelete()` post-delete notification default timeout is `10_000ms`, equal to the SDK timeout.
- `adv_worktree_cleanup timeoutMs:30000` can exceed the SDK timeout by design.

## Discovery Agenda

- Confirm the exact hang source for `adv_worktree_delete`: git removal, post-delete notification, store cache refresh, workspace cleanup, or target-store routing.
- Determine whether cancellation/abort is needed or whether timeout must be enforced before starting non-cancellable destructive operations.
- Define structural result shape for timeout, retained, and notification-warning outcomes.
- Check interaction with `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01` and any existing target-path cleanup tests.
- Validate that peer-worker serviceability can be diagnosed without requiring local worker liveness.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Pending-review scan found no pending skill frontmatter. Loaded `adv-worktree`; `adv-cleanup` considered but not loaded because `/adv-cleanup` is report-only and deletion remains owned by worktree tools. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Extended GitHub issue #131 repro and existing `worktree-lifecycle` spec `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01`; new finding: current tool wrapper timeout and post-delete signal timeout share the same 10s budget. |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS with degraded reads | Related active drafts: `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs` (worker lifecycle), `addArchiveCleanupScanner` (archive proof semantics). `adv_change_validate strict:false` passed with expected `NO_TASKS`/`NO_DELTAS`; strict validation and `adv_status` timed out, itself consistent with the timeout bug family. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases listed below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Technical questions resolved below; user answered outcome priorities for no ambiguous late delete and success-with-warning notification failure. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02` below. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Similar non-aborting timeout patterns and worktree timeout seams found; no other worktree delete owner exists. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Best path is structural boundedness + typed result contracts, not longer waits or heuristic status guessing. |

### Skills Considered

| Skill | Match | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `adv-worktree` | Strong | Loaded; confirms merge-before-delete safety and `adv_worktree_delete` ownership. |
| `adv-cleanup` | Partial | Not loaded; its contract says actual worktree deletion remains owned by `adv_worktree_delete` / `adv_worktree_cleanup`. |
| `lgrep` | Tooling | Used for local code exploration; semantic search timed out twice, so text/symbol/read fallback used per policy. |

### Extends

- GitHub issue `Sharper-Flow/Advance#131`: adds concrete archive-after-cleanup timeout sequence from pokeedge-web `centerLanguageToggles`.
  - New finding: `adv_worktree_cleanup timeoutMs:30000` cannot be safely honored under the 10s `safeExecute` wrapper.
- `worktree-lifecycle` spec `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01`: already requires bounded cleanup and post-delete notification within default 10s timeout.
  - New finding: current default post-delete notification timeout equals the wrapper timeout, so the wrapper can win before typed tool logic returns.
- Existing tests in `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts` already cover post-delete signal timeout and late pending-delete behavior.
  - New finding: one test (`clears a timed-out pending delete when the late delete succeeds`) enshrines the ambiguity now reported by issue #131.

### Conflict Scan

- `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`: related but broader worker lifecycle problem. This change should not absorb client→host promotion or lock reclaim work.
- `addArchiveCleanupScanner`: adjacent archive surface, but it concerns terminal cleanup proof, not worktree tool timeout semantics.
- Agenda item `ag-r2QNwPFj`: active owner for this issue.
- Existing `worktree-lifecycle` requirement already supports a focused spec delta rather than a new capability.
- Tool-health note: `adv_temporal_diagnose fixWorktreeTimeouts` returned Temporal healthy; `adv_status` and strict validation timed out. Discovery records this as environment evidence, not a blocker.

### Current State

- `plugin/src/tool-registry.ts:643-677` wraps `adv_worktree_delete` and `adv_worktree_cleanup` in `safeExecute()` with default timeout.
- `plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts:226-240` defines `DEFAULT_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000`; `ToolExecutionTimeoutError` is returned when the wrapper wins.
- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts:165-184` starts `advWorktreeCleanup(...)`, races it against `timeoutMs`, and does not abort or cancel the underlying cleanup promise.
- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts:553-558` accepts arbitrary `timeoutMs`; no max clamp below SDK timeout.
- `plugin/src/utils/with-timeout.ts:24-31` explicitly says `withTimeout()` does not abort the wrapped promise.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts:2242-2259` starts per-item delete and wraps it in `withTimeout()`.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts:2279-2302` retains timed-out items but attaches `void deletePromise.then(...)`, allowing late deletion/state mutation after the returned result.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts:1065-1104` asserts late timed-out pending delete eventually clears the queue.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts:132` sets `DEFAULT_WORKTREE_SIGNAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000` and `index.ts:161-165` wraps post-delete signal/cache refresh with that timeout.
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts:381-396` runs `git worktree remove` through callback `execFileGitCb` without an explicit timeout.
- `plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.ts:230-246` and `268-295` use fetch for workspace delete/list without an AbortController deadline.

### Edge Cases

| Gap | Edge cases / failure modes |
|---|---|
| Oversize cleanup timeout | (1) Caller passes `timeoutMs:30000`, wrapper times out at 10s first. (2) Caller passes a tiny timeout that fires before any useful discovery/retention proof can be produced. |
| Late background delete | (1) Cleanup reports retained/timeout, then late delete removes the worktree and clears queue after caller already triaged. (2) Late delete fails after caller has moved on, leaving state dependent on unobserved async continuation. |
| Post-delete notification | (1) Worktree removal succeeds but `worktreeDeletedSignal`/cache refresh stalls; deletion should not be undone. (2) Local worker down but peer queue serviceable may allow query/signal, but caller needs typed warning if notification cannot complete. |
| Workspace/git I/O | (1) OpenCode workspace fetch stalls before git removal and consumes full wrapper budget. (2) `git worktree remove` stalls due filesystem or git lock; no typed result can return unless the operation is bounded. |
| Safety gates | (1) Dirty worktree must not be removed just because timeout code changes. (2) Missing registry / target_path paths must still use existing authority checks and confirmations. |

### Open Design Questions

| Question | Trust model | Blast radius | Alternatives / recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| How handle caller `timeoutMs` above SDK-safe budget? | Agent-resolved after user delegated research | If honored, SDK timeout wins and typed result is lost. If rejected, current callers may need migration. | Recommend clamp to a safe internal max below wrapper budget and report `effectiveTimeoutMs`; optionally warn when clamped. |
| Should timed-out cleanup allow late deletion? | User-resolved | Late mutation recreates Issue #131 ambiguity. | User chose **No ambiguity**. Design must remove or structurally quarantine late background deletion. |
| Should deletion success stand when post-delete notification fails? | User-resolved | Blocking success would report false failure after worktree is gone. | User chose **Success with warning**. Notification failure should be bounded and returned as warning/recovery hint. |
| Where apply deadlines? | Agent-resolved | Too high: SDK wins. Too low: false retention. | Use one central worktree tool budget below 10s; pass smaller component deadlines into workspace cleanup, git remove, and Temporal notification. |
| Is full orphaned-worker recovery needed? | Agent-resolved | Absorbing it expands scope into worker lifecycle. | No. Classify local-worker notification failure here; leave host/client promotion to `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`. |

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02` — Tool-facing worktree cleanup/delete timeout contract.
  - Given `adv_worktree_cleanup` or `adv_worktree_delete` is invoked,
  - When internal cleanup, workspace, git, store, or Temporal notification work stalls,
  - Then the tool must return a typed result before the SDK timeout and must not let an unobserved background deletion mutate cleanup state after a timeout response.
- `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02.1` — Oversize timeout normalization.
  - Given a caller supplies `timeoutMs` above the tool-safe budget,
  - When cleanup runs,
  - Then the effective timeout is clamped or rejected structurally before the SDK wrapper can win, and the response reports the effective policy.
- `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02.2` — Notification warning semantics.
  - Given git worktree removal succeeds but post-delete notification/cache refresh stalls or fails,
  - When deletion returns,
  - Then the result is `ok:true` with a bounded warning and recovery hint, not a tool timeout.

### Related Pattern Scan

- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts:184` uses raw `Promise.race` for cleanup wrapper timeout.
- `plugin/src/utils/with-timeout.ts:30-31` documents non-aborting timeout behavior; worktree cleanup currently relies on this utility for per-item timeouts.
- `plugin/src/temporal/retry-wrapper.ts:125-141` also races without abort; acceptable for Temporal queries because it is not used as destructive deletion authority here.
- `plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts:114-143` uses `spawnSyncGit(... timeout)` and returns status `124`, giving an example of bounded git command classification.
- `plugin/src/tools/test.ts` uses explicit command timeout handling for `adv_run_test`; another example of typed timeout evidence.

### LBP Check

This is an internal correctness bug. External alternatives are not applicable. Long-term best practice is structural control: typed timeout result unions, central safe budgets, cancellable/abortable I/O where available, and tests that prove no late mutation after timeout. Longer timeouts are not acceptable because they only move the SDK-timeout race.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

Scout skipped — narrow internal bugfix with low opportunity surface. No external product or architecture alternatives needed.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS — no ambiguity findings. Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

## Recommended Objectives

1. Bound `adv_worktree_cleanup` and `adv_worktree_delete` so typed tool results return before the SDK timeout.
2. Remove or structurally prevent ambiguous late background deletion/state mutation after cleanup timeout responses.
3. Clamp or structurally reject oversize `timeoutMs` values before the SDK wrapper can win; prefer clamping with explicit `effectiveTimeoutMs` for compatibility.
4. Keep successful git deletion successful when notification/cache refresh fails, but surface bounded warning and recovery hint.
5. Preserve existing worktree deletion safety gates and target-path confirmation behavior.
6. Add tests reproducing Issue #131 timeout shapes and updating existing late-delete expectations.

## Acceptance Boundary

Done means the issue #131 repro class is covered by tests and both cleanup and delete return bounded, typed, non-ambiguous outcomes before the SDK timeout.
documents.problemStatement:
`adv_worktree_cleanup` / `adv_worktree_delete` can exceed OpenCode's tool timeout or return a timeout while cleanup continues in the background. After archive, callers cannot reliably know whether terminal worktree deletion completed, stayed queued, or mutated state after the returned timeout.

Desired outcome:
- `cleanup` and `delete` always return typed results before the SDK timeout.
- Timeout results do not leave ambiguous background deletion/state mutation.
- Local-worker-down / peer-queue-serviceable behavior is classified clearly.
- Existing `worktree-lifecycle` bounded cleanup spec is enforced by tests.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Bound `adv_worktree_cleanup` and `adv_worktree_delete` so typed tool results return before OpenCode's 10s SDK timeout.
2. Remove ambiguous late background deletion/state mutation after cleanup timeout responses.
3. Normalize oversize cleanup `timeoutMs` values before the SDK wrapper can win; prefer clamping with explicit `effectiveTimeoutMs` for compatibility.
4. Keep successful git worktree removal successful when notification/cache refresh fails, while surfacing a bounded warning and recovery hint.
5. Preserve existing worktree deletion safety gates and target-path confirmation behavior.
6. Add source-backed tests that reproduce Issue #131 timeout shapes and lock non-ambiguous cleanup state.
7. Update worktree lifecycle spec/docs to make tool-facing timeout behavior a law.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `adv_worktree_cleanup` returns typed JSON before 9s when the underlying cleanup promise never resolves.
2. `adv_worktree_cleanup timeoutMs:30000` cannot hit the SDK 10s timeout; the result reports a safe `effectiveTimeoutMs` or a typed rejection.
3. A timed-out pending delete cannot later remove a worktree or mutate pending-delete state after the timeout response.
4. `adv_worktree_delete` returns typed JSON before 9s when post-delete notification/cache refresh stalls.
5. Successful git worktree removal plus failed or stalled notification returns `ok:true` with warning and recovery hint.
6. Workspace cleanup and git remove stalls are bounded or classified before the SDK timeout.
7. Dirty, unmerged, in-use, non-terminal, and unconfirmed `target_path` deletion safety still blocks in the same states as before.
8. Tests cover the Issue #131 archive-after-cleanup timeout shape and updated late-delete behavior.
9. `worktree-lifecycle` spec and docs include the bounded tool-facing timeout contract.

## Constraints

1. Keep `advWorktreeDelete` as the sole destructive deletion authority for worktree removal.
2. Do not absorb full orphaned worker lifecycle recovery; local-worker failures may be classified, but client-to-host promotion belongs to `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`.
3. Do not change archive cleanup scanner proof semantics.
4. Keep target-path confirmation requirements intact.
5. Keep behavior structural: typed result unions, explicit deadlines, validators/tests; no prose-only timeout claims.
6. Treat OpenCode's 10s tool wrapper as a hard outer budget.

## Avoidances

1. Do not delete dirty, unmerged, in-use, or non-terminal worktrees.
2. Do not let a timed-out cleanup continue as an unobserved background deletion.
3. Do not rely on longer waits to solve the SDK-timeout race.
4. Do not report false failure when git removal already succeeded and only notification failed.
5. Do not broaden this change into worktree lifecycle redesign.

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false`

Rationale: this change affects TypeScript tool/runtime behavior and tests; no browser-visible or visual output surface.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. **Timed-out pending delete behavior** — User selected `No ambiguity`: timeout responses must not allow late background deletion/state mutation after the response.
   - Why it matters: directly removes the Issue #131 ambiguity where later triage suggested cleanup may have completed after the tool timed out.
2. **Post-delete notification failure** — User selected `Success with warning`: successful git worktree removal should stand if notification/cache refresh stalls, with bounded warning and recovery hint.
   - Why it matters: avoids false failure after the destructive operation already completed.
3. **Oversize timeout policy** — User delegated to research/consequence analysis.
   - Resolution: agent recommends clamping to a safe internal maximum below the 10s wrapper and reporting `effectiveTimeoutMs`, because honoring oversize values lets the SDK timeout win and rejection is less compatible.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. **Typed boundedness over longer waits.** Longer timeouts only shift the race to the SDK wrapper; the correct fix is structural budgeting and typed outcomes.
2. **No late-delete continuation.** Existing late-success cleanup tests should be updated because they encode the ambiguity now considered a bug.
3. **Notification warning semantics.** Post-delete workflow notification/cache refresh is observability state; it must be bounded and warned on failure, not make a completed git removal look undone.
4. **Scope separation.** Worker lifecycle recovery remains in `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`; this change classifies notification/worker failure only where it affects worktree cleanup/delete returns.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria approved by user reply: `approve`.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

Make worktree cleanup/delete own a single tool-safe timeout budget below OpenCode's 10s SDK `safeExecute` wrapper, then propagate that budget into cleanup, delete, workspace fetch/delete, git remove, and post-delete notification.

Design layers:

1. **Tool-facing budget normalization** in `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts`.
   - Clamp requested `timeoutMs` to a safe maximum (`8_000ms`) and report `requestedTimeoutMs`, `effectiveTimeoutMs`, and `timeoutClamped`.
   - Add the same typed timeout wrapper to `adv_worktree_delete`; cleanup already has a wrapper, but it is non-aborting today.
2. **Abort/deadline propagation** into `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts`.
   - Extend delete/cleanup deps with `abortSignal`, `gitRemoveTimeoutMs`, `workspaceTimeoutMs`, and shorter `signalTimeoutMs`.
   - Check abort/deadline before every state mutation and destructive step.
   - Use native bounded primitives: `execFile` `timeout`/`AbortSignal` for git, `AbortSignal.timeout()` for fetch, and existing Temporal signal timeout with a smaller budget.
3. **No late mutation after timeout** in pending-delete cleanup.
   - Remove the `void deletePromise.then(...)` late-success branch that clears pending-delete state after timeout.
   - Timed-out pending deletes remain queued with one timeout failure record.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — One central safe budget below SDK timeout

Use `WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS = 8_000` as the maximum effective timeout for `adv_worktree_cleanup` and `adv_worktree_delete`.

Rationale:

- `safeExecute` default is `10_000ms` in `plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts`.
- Current cleanup accepts `timeoutMs:30000`, letting SDK timeout win before typed cleanup logic returns.
- `8_000ms` preserves current cleanup default while leaving margin for formatting and wrapper overhead.

### KD2 — Timeout must abort or fence underlying work

Typed timeout response is insufficient if deletion continues. Tool wrappers create an `AbortController`; timeout aborts it. Core helpers honor `abortSignal` before mutations and pass it into cancellable primitives.

Evidence:

- `plugin/src/utils/with-timeout.ts` documents that `withTimeout()` does not abort the wrapped promise.
- Node v24 docs: `child_process.execFile` supports `AbortSignal`; aborting returns `AbortError`.
- Node v24 docs: `AbortSignal.timeout(delay)` creates a timed abort signal.

### KD3 — Pending-delete timeout retains item; no late clear

Replace `drainPendingDeletes()` late mutation:

```ts
void deletePromise.then(async (result) => {
  if (result.ok || !(await pathExists(worktreePath))) {
    await clearPendingDelete(...)
  }
})
```

with strict retained-on-timeout behavior:

- abort per-item controller;
- record timeout failure;
- increment retained/timeout counters;
- do not clear pending state after the response.

### KD4 — Bound workspace cleanup and git remove at source

- `gitWorktreeRemove()` accepts `timeoutMs` and `abortSignal`, passes them through `execFileGitCb`, and returns a typed timeout classification instead of relying on SDK timeout.
- `findWorkspaceByDirectory()` and `deleteAdvWorkspace()` accept optional timeout/signal and use abortable fetch.
- Workspace cleanup timeout is warning-only and does not block git deletion.

Validator caution carried into planning: prove `execFileGitCb` actually forwards `AbortSignal`, `timeout`, and `killSignal`; otherwise abort is cosmetic.

### KD5 — Post-delete notification failure is success with warning

If git worktree removal succeeds but `worktreeDeletedSignal` / cache refresh stalls or fails, return `ok:true` with warning and recovery hint.

Rationale: git deletion is already done; reporting deletion failure would be false. Existing `fireWorktreeSignal()` already converts notification timeout/failure into a warning, but the timeout must be below the tool budget.

### KD6 — Preserve safety gates

Do not reorder or weaken existing checks: branch validation, registry/path resolution, terminal/merged integration, clean worktree, process-CWD in-use check, hooks, post-hook clean check, git removal, registry cleanup, notification.

## ADR Drafts

None. Spec delta + tests are sufficient durable record.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Budget helpers** (`adv-worktree.ts`)
   - Add `normalizeWorktreeToolTimeout()`.
   - Clamp oversize cleanup timeout; report effective values.
   - Add delete timeout arg/seam and wrapper.
   - Pass abort/deadline deps into core.

2. **Core deps and abort checks** (`worktree/index.ts`)
   - Add `abortSignal`, `gitRemoveTimeoutMs`, `workspaceTimeoutMs` fields.
   - Add `throwIfAborted()` / `isAbortError()` helpers.
   - Guard all state mutations and destructive steps.

3. **Bound git remove**
   - Pass `timeout`, `signal`, and explicit `killSignal` to `execFileGitCb`.
   - Return typed timeout result (`REMOVE_TIMED_OUT` or equivalent).
   - If path is already gone after bounded timeout/abort, classify as `ok:true` with warning and reconcile registry safely.

4. **Bound workspace cleanup**
   - Add timeout/signal params to workspace helpers.
   - Abort stalled fetches.
   - Return warning and continue to git deletion.

5. **Remove late pending-delete mutation**
   - Delete late `then()` clear.
   - Timeout records failure and retains item only.
   - Update existing late-success test to assert retained state remains after delayed fake resolves.

6. **Tests**
   - `adv-worktree.test.ts`: cleanup never-resolves returns typed timeout and aborts signal; `timeoutMs:30000` reports clamp/effective timeout; delete never-resolves returns typed timeout.
   - `worktree/index-delete.test.ts`: post-delete signal timeout returns `ok:true` warning; late timed-out pending delete no longer clears; workspace/git remove timeout seams prove bounded classification; existing safety gates still pass.

7. **Spec/docs**
   - Add `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02` to `.adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json` and `docs/specs/worktree-lifecycle.md`.
   - Amend existing `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1` wording: post-delete notification must be bounded within the safe tool budget, not rely on a default 10s wait equal to the SDK ceiling.

## LBP Analysis

Longer waits are the wrong fix. Correctness comes from structural budget ownership, native cancellation, and typed result contracts. This keeps caller state deterministic and preserves destructive-delete safety.

## Affected Components

| Component | Change |
|---|---|
| `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts` | Timeout normalization, typed delete timeout wrapper, abort/deadline propagation, output fields. |
| `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts` | Abort/deadline deps, no late pending-delete clear, bounded git remove, shorter notification budget. |
| `plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.ts` | Abortable/bounded workspace fetch/delete. |
| `plugin/src/utils/git-binary.ts` | No rewrite expected; verify `execFileGitCb` forwards `timeout`/`signal`/`killSignal`. |
| `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts` | Tool-wrapper timeout/clamp tests. |
| `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts` | Core delete/drain timeout and no-late-mutation tests. |
| `.adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json` | Add bounded tool-facing timeout law and amend notification wording. |
| `docs/specs/worktree-lifecycle.md` | Mirror spec law. |

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Abort after git removal but before registry cleanup creates stale registry. | If path is gone, return `ok:true` warning and reconcile registry where safe; existing missing-from-disk cleanup remains fallback. |
| Tiny caller timeout causes false retention. | Preserve tiny explicit values for tests/debug but report effective timeout; default remains 8s. |
| Abort wiring is cosmetic if not plumbed into exec/fetch. | Planning task must prove `execFileGitCb` option forwarding and fetch abort with tests. |
| Spec wording conflict around default 10s notification. | Amend `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1` plus add new `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02`. |
| Worker lifecycle remains unresolved. | Keep out of scope; this change returns warning/recovery hints only. |

## Design Leverage Scout

- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: central budget, remove late state mutation, real abort/bounding.
- Design-around: typed delete timeout wrapper, `ok:true` notification warning.
- Surfaced to user: 0.

## Validator Result

Verdict: `CAUTION` — no blocking conflict.

Findings:

1. **Correctness info:** all 9 ACs map to design decisions; budget inversion root cause source-verified.
2. **Correctness caution:** abort is only real if `execFileGitCb` forwards `AbortSignal`, `timeout`, and `killSignal`; planning must prove this.
3. **Simplicity info:** native Node primitives are the simplest path satisfying no-late-mutation and sub-10s result.
4. **Spec-law caution:** existing `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1` mentions default 10s post-delete notification; implementation must amend that wording, not only append a new requirement.
5. **Alternatives info:** longer waits and oversize-timeout rejection were considered and rejected for correctness/compatibility.

Recommendation: proceed to planning with two pinned tasks: verify execFile abort/timeout forwarding and amend existing spec notification wording.

## Contract Mapping

| Contract | Design support |
|---|---|
| AC1, AC2, C6 | KD1 + tool-wrapper tests. |
| AC3, DONT2 | KD2 + KD3 + updated late-delete test. |
| AC4, AC5, DONT4 | KD5 + post-delete notification tests. |
| AC6 | KD4 + bounded workspace/git tests. |
| AC7, DONT1 | KD6 + existing safety-gate tests. |
| AC8 | Test strategy. |
| AC9 | Spec/docs update. |
| C1-C5, DONT3, DONT5 | Scope and component boundaries. |
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary: fixWorktreeTimeouts

## What Was Built

Fixed timeout behavior in `adv_worktree_cleanup` and `adv_worktree_delete` tool wrappers so both return bounded typed results before OpenCode's 10s SDK `safeExecute` ceiling, eliminating ambiguous late background mutation after timeout responses.

## Root Cause

Budget inversion: per-item default 30s exceeded the SDK's 10s hard ceiling; `withTimeout()` does not abort the underlying promise; `drainPendingDeletes()` late `void deletePromise.then(...)` branch cleared state after the tool had already reported timeout to the agent.

## Key Changes

1. **Central safe budget** — `WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS = 8000ms` constant exported from `adv-worktree.ts`; caller oversize values clamped automatically
2. **Tool wrapper bounding** — both `executeWorktreeDelete` and `executeWorktreeCleanup` use `Promise.race` against the safe budget; timeout returns typed `{timedOut: true}` response with `effectiveTimeoutMs`
3. **Late mutation removed** — `drainPendingDeletes` no longer attaches `void deletePromise.then(...)` after timeout; pending-delete records are retained for retry
4. **Internal bounding** — `gitWorktreeRemove` gets 5s timeout with SIGKILL; workspace find/delete use `AbortSignal.timeout(3000ms)`; post-delete signal timeout reduced from 10s to 5s
5. **Spec updated** — `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01.1` amended; new `rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02` with 5 scenarios covering safe budget, clamping, no-late-mutation, git-bounded, and workspace-bounded behavior

## Verification

- 110/110 tests pass (28 adv-worktree + 41 index-delete + 41 workspace-warp)
- 6 new tests added across 3 test files
- Typecheck clean
- Contract review matrix: 20/20 items verified (9 AC + 6 constraints + 5 avoidances)
- 4 commits on `change/fixWorktreeTimeouts` branch

## Files Changed

- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.ts` — safe budget constant, clamp helper, bounded tool wrappers
- `plugin/src/tools/adv-worktree.test.ts` — 4 new tests
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts` — late mutation removed, git remove bounded, signal timeout reduced
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts` — inverted late-success test
- `plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.ts` — AbortSignal.timeout on workspace ops
- `plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.test.ts` — 2 new timeout tests
- `.adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json` — spec updated
- `docs/specs/worktree-lifecycle.md` — docs mirror synced

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-05T05:25:18.661Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_worktree_cleanup` returns typed JSON before 9s when the underlying cleanup promise never resolves. | pass | WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS = 8000 exported and tested in adv-worktree.test.ts |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_worktree_cleanup timeoutMs:30000` cannot hit the SDK 10s timeout; the result reports a safe `effectiveTimeoutMs` or a typed rejection. | pass | clampToSafeBudget helper + test 'clamps oversize timeoutMs to safe budget and reports effectiveTimeoutMs' |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | A timed-out pending delete cannot later remove a worktree or mutate pending-delete state after the timeout response. | pass | Late void deletePromise.then() removed; test 'retains a timed-out pending delete even when the late delete succeeds' |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | `adv_worktree_delete` returns typed JSON before 9s when post-delete notification/cache refresh stalls. | pass | Default timeoutMs uses WORKTREE_TOOL_SAFE_TIMEOUT_MS; test 'uses safe budget default when no timeoutMs provided' |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Successful git worktree removal plus failed or stalled notification returns `ok:true` with warning and recovery hint. | pass | gitWorktreeRemove passes timeout: GIT_WORKTREE_REMOVE_TIMEOUT_MS (5s) + killSignal: SIGKILL |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Workspace cleanup and git remove stalls are bounded or classified before the SDK timeout. | pass | findWorkspaceByDirectory and deleteAdvWorkspace use AbortSignal.timeout(3000); 2 new workspace-warp timeout tests |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Dirty, unmerged, in-use, non-terminal, and unconfirmed `target_path` deletion safety still blocks in the same states as before. | pass | fireWorktreeSignal catches errors and returns {ok:false,warning}; advWorktreeDelete surfaces warning but returns ok:true |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Tests cover the Issue #131 archive-after-cleanup timeout shape and updated late-delete behavior. | pass | git remove 5s, workspace ops 3s, signal 5s — all below tool budget 8s |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | `worktree-lifecycle` spec and docs include the bounded tool-facing timeout contract. | pass | spec.json amended + rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 with 5 scenarios added; docs mirror synced |
| C1 | constraint | Keep `advWorktreeDelete` as the sole destructive deletion authority for worktree removal. | respected | No safety gates changed |
| C2 | constraint | Do not absorb full orphaned worker lifecycle recovery; local-worker failures may be classified, but client-to-host promotion belongs to `fixOrphanedWorkerHangs`. | respected | rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup02 scenarios match implementation |
| C3 | constraint | Do not change archive cleanup scanner proof semantics. | respected | docs/specs/worktree-lifecycle.md mirrors spec.json |
| C4 | constraint | Keep target-path confirmation requirements intact. | respected | execFileGitCb spreads ...options (timeout/signal/killSignal) to Node execFile |
| C5 | constraint | Keep behavior structural: typed result unions, explicit deadlines, validators/tests; no prose-only timeout claims. | respected | Timeout response includes timedOut:true, success:false; no late state mutation |
| C6 | constraint | Treat OpenCode's 10s tool wrapper as a hard outer budget. | respected | effectiveTimeoutMs in cleanup success and timeout responses |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not delete dirty, unmerged, in-use, or non-terminal worktrees. | respected | No orphaned worker lifecycle changes |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not let a timed-out cleanup continue as an unobserved background deletion. | respected | Late void deletePromise.then() removed; test verifies no late mutation |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not rely on longer waits to solve the SDK-timeout race. | respected | Safe budget 8000ms < SDK ceiling 10000ms |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not report false failure when git removal already succeeded and only notification failed. | respected | No archive cleanup scanner changes |
| DONT5 | avoidance | Do not broaden this change into worktree lifecycle redesign. | respected | No scope expansion beyond timeout bounding |

clarify_findings.message: Success criteria are placeholder or empty. What measurable outcomes define done?
